channel.title: MIKE Research Clusters
channel.description: Recent content on MIKE Research Clusters

item.title: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Suppawong Tuarob
item.description: Assoc. Prof. Dr. Suppawong Tuarob is an Associate Professor and the Head of the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering Research Clusters (MIKE). Recently recognized among the Top 100 Researchers, his work fundamentally targets the intersection of multi-disciplinary AI applications. Education Ph.D. in Computer Science and Engineering, The Pennsylvania State University M.S. in Science, Industrial Engineering, The Pennsylvania State University B.S. in Computer Science and Engineering, University of Michigan

item.title: Call for Proposals: 2026 Google Awards for ML Research and Education with TPUs
item.description: Unlock Funding and Compute for Your ML Research! Google has officially announced its 2026 Awards for Machine Learning Research and Education with TPUs! This is an incredible opportunity for our lab members to secure significant funding and top-tier computing power. Google is inviting proposals to advance machine learning through foundational research, applied science, and the integration of Google TPUs into educational courses. What is the Award? Selected proposals will receive a fantastic support package:

item.title: Congratulations to Assoc. Prof. Dr. Suppawong Tuarob on Being Named a Top 100 Researcher in Thailand!
item.description: The MIKE Research Clusters proudly congratulates our Principal Investigator, Assoc. Prof. Dr. Suppawong Tuarob, on his remarkable recognition as one of the Top 100 Researchers in Thailand.

item.title: MIKE Lab Team Dinner: Enjoying Korean BBQ at Baan Dee
item.description: The MIKE Research Clusters recently gathered for a delightful team dinner at Baan Dee to enjoy some authentic Korean BBQ and celebrate our recent milestones.

item.title: XD-LDL: Improving Generalization in Acne Severity Classification via Cross-Dataset Label Distribution Learning
item.description: Abstract This study introduces a novel approach to enhance acne severity classification by leveraging cross-dataset training within a Label Distribution Learning (LDL) framework. Traditional acne grading methods are often subjective and inconsistent, while automated systems struggle to generalize across diverse datasets, limiting their real-world applicability. This research addresses this challenge by integrating data from multiple sources (Acne04 and AcneSCU) to train a robust model capable of accurately classifying acne severity despite variations in image quality, lighting, and skin tone.

item.title: Artificial intelligence in computer programming education: A systematic literature review
item.description: Abstract The demand for skilled programmers and the increasing complexity of coding skills have led to a rise in the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) technologies in computer programming education. Previous research has explored the potential of AI in aspects such as grading assignments, generating feedback, detecting plagiarism, and identifying at-risk students, but there is a lack of systematic reviews focused on AI-powered teaching processes in computer programming classes[cite: 1576].

item.title: Leveraging Movement Representation from Contrastive Learning for Asteroid Detection
item.description: Abstract Asteroid detection is a critical task in astronomy for planetary defense and understanding the solar system’s evolution. Traditional methods often struggle with low signal-to-noise ratios and the vast amount of data generated by modern sky surveys. This study introduces a novel deep learning approach that leverages movement representation learned through contrastive learning to improve the accuracy and efficiency of asteroid detection. By training the model to recognize the specific spatiotemporal patterns of moving objects against a static stellar background, the proposed framework enhances the ability to distinguish true asteroids from artifacts and noise.

item.title: "Paper, Meet Code": A Deep Learning Approach to Linking Scholarly Articles With GitHub Repositories
item.description: Abstract Computer scientists often publish their source code accompanying their publications, prominently using code repositories across various domains. Despite the concurrent existence of scholarly articles and their associated official code repositories, explicit references linking the two are often missing. Traditionally, identifying whether scholarly content and code repositories pertain to the same research project requires manual inspection, a time-consuming task. This paper proposes a deep learning-based algorithm for automatically matching scholarly articles with their corresponding official code repositories.

item.title: TeReKG: A temporal collaborative knowledge graph framework for software team recommendation
item.description: Abstract Successful software development requires a cohesive team with the right mix of technical skills and the ability to collaborate effectively. However, forming a software team that can execute tasks with precision and efficiency requires a deep understanding of each member’s competence, experience, and cooperation history. Previously, automated software team selection has evaluated technical skills, cohesion, and cooperation history. However, the previous method had some limitations. Particularly, local features directly calculated from team members were subjective to the researchers' views, and the method ignored the temporal aspect of open-source software development.

item.title: CAMELON: A System for Crime Metadata Extraction and Spatiotemporal Visualization From Online News Articles
item.description: Abstract Online news articles serve as a primary source of information for understanding social phenomena, including criminal activities. However, the unstructured nature of news text makes it difficult to systematically analyze and visualize crime patterns across time and space. This paper presents CAMELON, an end-to-end system designed for the automated extraction of crime metadata and spatiotemporal visualization from online news articles. The system utilizes advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to identify key crime attributes such as crime types, locations, and timestamps from Thai news reports.

item.title: Artificial Intelligence in Cosmetic Dermatology: A Systematic Literature Review
item.description: Abstract Over the last ten years, the field of dermatology has experienced significant advancements through the utilization of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies. The adoption of such technologies is multifaceted, encompassing tasks such as screening, diagnosis, treatment, and prediction of treatment outcomes. The majority of prior systematic reviews in this domain were centered on medical dermatology, with the aim of detecting and managing serious skin diseases such as skin cancer. However, the adoption of AI in cosmetic dermatology, which focuses on improving skin conditions for cosmetic purposes, has not been comprehensively reviewed.

item.title: FALCON: Detecting and classifying abusive language in social networks using context features and unlabeled data
item.description: Abstract Social networks have grown into a widespread form of communication that allows a large number of users to participate in conversations and consume information at any time. The casual nature of social media allows for nonstandard terminology, some of which may be considered rude and derogatory. As a result, a significant portion of social media users is found to express disrespectful language. This problem may intensify in certain developing countries where young children are granted unsupervised access to social media platforms.

item.title: Ar Kar
item.description: Biography I am a Master’s student in Computer Science (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning) at Mahidol University, Faculty of ICT, and working on AI-driven educational technologies. My research focuses on learning analytics, student behavioral analysis, and AI-powered teaching assistants that enhance learning outcomes.

item.title: Assistant Professor Dr. Akara Supratak
item.description: Assistant Professor Dr. Akara Supratak is a researcher at the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering (MIKE) Research Clusters at Mahidol University. Biography Assistant Professor Dr. Akara Supratak’s research primarily focuses on biomedical informatics and deep learning, with specific applications in medical image and signal analysis, including automated sleep stage scoring. Education Ph.D. in Computing Research, Imperial College London, United Kingdom M.Sc. in Computing, Imperial College London, United Kingdom B.Sc. in Information and Communication Technology, Mahidol University, Thailand

item.title: Assistant Professor Dr. Jidapa Kraisangka
item.description: Assistant Professor Dr. Jidapa Kraisangka is a researcher at the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering (MIKE) Research Clusters at Mahidol University. Biography Assistant Professor Dr. Jidapa Kraisangka is the Assistant Dean for Academic and Curriculum Development at the Faculty of ICT, Mahidol University. Her research focuses on artificial intelligence and decision support systems, specifically probabilistic graphical models. Education Ph.D. in Information Science, University of Pittsburgh, USA M.S. in Information Science, University of Pittsburgh, USA B.

item.title: Assistant Professor Dr. Siripen Pongpaichet
item.description: Assistant Professor Dr. Siripen Pongpaichet is a researcher at the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering (MIKE) Research Clusters at Mahidol University. Biography Assistant Professor Dr. Siripen Pongpaichet is a faculty member and administrator at the Faculty of ICT, Mahidol University. Her research focuses on data science and intelligent systems, specifically situation recognition and event streams processing. Education Ph.D. in Computer Science, University of California, Irvine (UCI), USA M.S. in Computer Science, University of California, Irvine (UCI), USA B.

item.title: Assistant Professor Dr. Thanapon Noraset
item.description: Assistant Professor Dr. Thanapon Noraset is a researcher at the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering (MIKE) Research Clusters at Mahidol University. Biography Assistant Professor Dr. Thanapon Noraset is the Assistant Dean for Academic Development and Courses at the Faculty of ICT, Mahidol University. His research primarily focuses on Natural Language Processing (NLP) and machine learning applications. Education Ph.D. in Computer Science, Northwestern University, USA M.S. in Computer Science, Northwestern University, USA B.

item.title: Assistant Professor Dr. Tipajin Thaipisutikul
item.description: Assistant Professor Dr. Tipajin Thaipisutikul is a researcher at the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering (MIKE) Research Clusters and MVIT Research LAB at Mahidol University. Biography Assistant Professor Dr. Tipajin Thaipisutikul’s research primarily focuses on advanced artificial intelligence and data science, including deep learning, sequence learning, and applied intelligence. Education Ph.D. in Computer Science (1st Class Honor), National Central University (NCU), Taiwan M.Sc. in Information Technology (2nd Class Honor), University of Sydney (USYD), Australia B.

item.title: Assistant Professor Dr. Wudhichart Sawangphol
item.description: Assistant Professor Dr. Wudhichart Sawangphol is a researcher at the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering (MIKE) Research Clusters at Mahidol University. Biography Assistant Professor Dr. Wudhichart Sawangphol is the Assistant Dean for Research at the Faculty of ICT, Mahidol University. His research primarily focuses on data analytics, machine learning, and visualization for real-world decision-making systems. Education Ph.D. in Information Technology, Monash University, Australia Master of Information Technology, Monash University, Australia B.

item.title: Chawanagorn Sujintawong
item.description: Biography Master’s student in Computer Science interested in AI, machine learning, and data science. My work focuses on learning from incomplete tabular data using graph neural networks. I enjoy combining mathematical rigor with practical ML applications and am open to collaboration.

item.title: Dr. Petch Sajjacholapunt
item.description: Dr. Petch Sajjacholapunt is a researcher at the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering (MIKE) Research Clusters at Mahidol University. Biography Dr. Petch Sajjacholapunt is a Lecturer and currently serves as the Deputy Dean for Administration and Human Resources at the Faculty of ICT, Mahidol University. His research interests include Technology-Enhanced Learning, Recommendation Systems, and Image Processing. Education Ph.D. in Computer Science, University of Warwick, United Kingdom M.Phil. in Computer Science, The University of Manchester, United Kingdom M.

item.title: Dr. Sirawich Vachmanus
item.description: Dr. Sirawich Vachmanus is a researcher at the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering (MIKE) Research Clusters at Mahidol University. Biography Dr. Sirawich Vachmanus is a Lecturer in the Computer Science Academic Group at Mahidol University. His research primarily focuses on the intersection of artificial intelligence and robotics, including computer vision and sensor fusion for autonomous systems. Education Ph.D. in Human Mechanical Systems and Design Engineering, Hokkaido University, Japan M.E. in Human Mechanical Systems and Design Engineering, Hokkaido University, Japan B.

item.title: KRISANAKORN RUGBUMROONG
item.description: Biography

item.title: Noppachanin Kongsathitporn
item.description: Biography I am inevitable.

item.title: Pat Vatiwutipong
item.description: Biography My research centers on combining mathematical modeling with machine learning to generate robust statistical inferences from data. The focus of my work is explainable and interpretable AI, developing models that go beyond accurate prediction to reveal how data behaves, identify underlying patterns, and make the decision-making process of AI systems transparent and understandable, particularly in high-stakes domains like healthcare.

item.title: Phaphontee Yamchote
item.description: Biography I am a PhD student in Computer Science with research interests in mathematical logic, computational logic in AI, algorithmic machine learning, and data science. My current work focuses on learning representations of incomplete tabular data using graph neural networks. I am interested in research that combines mathematical rigor with practical machine learning applications, especially in problems involving structured data and feature interactions. I am open to collaboration with researchers and professionals in AI, machine learning, and related fields.

item.title: Pisut Manorat
item.description: Biography I am a PhD student working on causality in continuous-time event streams. My research focuses on investigating the assumptions for discovering causal structure from event streams and how to integrate the causal knowledge into machine learning models, allowing them to perform causal reasoning tasks. My research aims to facilitate the development of robust machine learning systems that can be used to support decision-making process in high-stake domains where experiments can be difficult to perform, e.

item.title: Prahyat Puangjaktha
item.description: Biography I am a PhD Student passionate about using data to understand environmental challenges. My work focuses on the intersection of Environmental Science and Data Science. Currently, I am studying to apply AI and Remote Sensing for Environmental Challenges and Disasters. I am driven to produce research that translates into actionable insights for environmental management in Thailand. I am always open to connecting with fellow researchers, data scientists, and environmental professionals.
